chinese_text: 殷契，母曰簡狄，有娀氏之女，為帝嚳次妃。
translation_text: Qi of Yin's mother was Jian Di, who was one of the daughters of Yousong and the secondary wife of Emperor Ku.
translation_text_raw: Qi of Yin's mother was Jian Di, who was one of the daughters of Yousong and the secondary wife of Emperor Ku.

chinese_text: 三人行浴，見玄鳥墮其卵，簡狄取吞之，因孕生契。
translation_text: She was going with her two sisters to bathe, when she saw a dark bird drop its egg.
translation_text_raw: She was going with her two sisters to bathe, when she saw a dark bird drop its egg.

translation_text: Jian Di picked it up, and swallowed it, and thus being with child gave birth to Qi.
translation_text_raw: Jian Di picked it up, and swallowed it, and thus being with child gave birth to Qi.

translation_text: When Qi grew up, he was successful in assisting Yu to control the flood, and the Emperor Shun, directing Qi, said:
translation_text_raw: When Qi grew up, he was successful in assisting Yu to control the flood, and the Emperor Shun, directing Qi, said:

chinese_text: 「百姓不親，五品不訓，汝為司徒而敬敷五教，五教在寬。
translation_text: The people are wanting in affection for one another, and do not observe the five orders of relationship.
translation_text_raw: The people are wanting in affection for one another, and do not observe the five orders of relationship.

translation_text: You, as Minister of Instruction, should reverently inculcate the lessons of duty belonging to those five orders, but do so with gentleness.
translation_text_raw: You, as Minister of Instruction, should reverently inculcate the lessons of duty belonging to those five orders, but do so with gentleness.

chinese_text: 契興於唐、虞、大禹之際，功業著於百姓，百姓以平。
translation_text: He held in fief the principality of Shang, and was given the Surname Of Zi.
translation_text_raw: He held in fief the principality of Shang, and was given the Surname Of Zi.

translation_text: Qi flourished in the reigns of Yao, Shun, and the great Yu.
translation_text_raw: Qi flourished in the reigns of Yao, Shun, and the great Yu.

translation_text: His services were manifest to the people, who were accordingly at peace.
translation_text_raw: His services were manifest to the people, who were accordingly at peace.

translation_text: Qi died, and His Son Zhaoming succeeded him.
translation_text_raw: Qi died, and His Son Zhaoming succeeded him.

translation_text: Zhaoming died, and His Son Xiangtu succeeded him.
translation_text_raw: Zhaoming died, and His Son Xiangtu succeeded him.

translation_text: Xiangtu died, and his Son Chang Ruo succeeded him.
translation_text_raw: Xiangtu died, and his Son Chang Ruo succeeded him.

translation_text: Chang Ruo died, and his Son Cao Yu succeeded him.
translation_text_raw: Chang Ruo died, and his Son Cao Yu succeeded him.

translation_text: Cao Yu died, and His Son Ming succeeded him.
translation_text_raw: Cao Yu died, and His Son Ming succeeded him.

translation_text: Ming died, and His Son Zhen succeeded him.
translation_text_raw: Ming died, and His Son Zhen succeeded him.

translation_text: Zhen died, and His Son Wei succeeded him.
translation_text_raw: Zhen died, and His Son Wei succeeded him.

translation_text: Wei died, and His Son Baoding succeeded him.
translation_text_raw: Wei died, and His Son Baoding succeeded him.
